refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as (total scheduled operating time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled operating time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts mission success and stakeholder confidence. Refinement clarifies the measurement basis (scheduled vs. unplanned downtime), establishes the quantitative target (≥0.97), and defines the evaluation window. This is critical for safety-critical heavy equipment where availability directly correlates with operational safety and productivity.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection and post-evaluation analysis: track equipment operational state (active, idle, maintenance, fault) via Telemetry interface; correlate with maintenance logs and incident reports; calculate availability ratio at evaluation period end; compare against ≥0.97 threshold.
sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The NGHE solution shall detect and report equipment faults and degradation in real-time via SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces.
FR-AVAIL-002: The NGHE solution shall minimize mean time to repair (MTTR) through predictive maintenance and rapid fault diagnostics.
FR-AVAIL-003: The NGHE solution shall provide redundancy and graceful degradation for critical subsystems to sustain operations during partial failures.
FR-AVAIL-004: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational readiness through scheduled preventive maintenance without exceeding planned downtime budgets.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement continuous health monitoring with predictive analytics and automated fault detection across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; couple with preventive maintenance scheduling and spare parts logistics.
DP-B: Establish redundant critical subsystems (dual sensors, failover controllers) and implement graceful degradation logic to sustain core operations during component failures.

DP-C: Integrate real-time telemetry collection, centralized diagnostics engine, and closed-loop maintenance workflow (detect → alert → schedule → execute → verify) across Safety Services and Vehicle modules.
DP-D: Combine DP-A, DP-B, and DP-C into a holistic availability architecture: continuous monitoring + redundancy + predictive maintenance + rapid response.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Holistic availability architecture combining continuous health monitoring (DP-A), redundancy and graceful degradation (DP-B), and integrated diagnostics and maintenance workflow (DP-C). This multi-faceted approach addresses the root causes of unavailability: undetected faults, single points of failure, and slow repair cycles.

potential_sources: Health monitoring (DP-A) must couple cleanly with redundancy logic (DP-B) to avoid false positives triggering unnecessary failovers.
Maintenance scheduling (DP-A) must coordinate with operational tasking (Tasking interface) to avoid scheduling maintenance during critical missions.
Telemetry data volume from continuous monitoring may stress Network Link capacity; buffering and compression strategies required.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composite design that integrates three complementary strategies. Coupling risks are manageable through clear interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) and architectural separation of concerns. No fundamental design contradiction identified.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Predictive analytics models can be trained on historical failure data; if data is sparse or unrepresentative, prediction accuracy will suffer.
ASSUMPTION: Redundant subsystems can be implemented without excessive cost/weight/complexity penalties; heavy equipment constraints may limit feasibility.
RISK: Network Link reliability is critical; if telemetry transmission fails, health monitoring is blind and availability target is at risk.
RISK: Maintenance scheduling conflicts with operational demands; if maintenance is deferred to meet production targets, availability will degrade.
RISK: Operator and tele-operator training on new diagnostics and maintenance workflows may introduce human error during transition.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly address Network Link resilience (e.g., local caching of telemetry, edge analytics on Vehicle) and operator training requirements. Consider phased rollout of predictive maintenance to build confidence and data quality before full deployment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating availability across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Owns the holistic health monitoring architecture, redundancy coordination logic, and maintenance workflow integration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional: NGHE Solution collects health telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; transmits aggregated diagnostics and maintenance recommendations back to Safety Services and external maintenance systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution generates and broadcasts safety-critical alerts (fault detection, redundancy activation, maintenance urgency) to Safety Services and operators.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operational tasking from Site Manager/Tele-operator; coordinates maintenance scheduling to avoid conflicts; may recommend task deferral if availability is at risk.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution depends on Network Link for telemetry transmission and remote diagnostics; must implement local resilience (edge caching, offline diagnostics) to tolerate Network Link degradation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of the holistic availability architecture. It integrates health monitoring, redundancy coordination, and maintenance workflows across all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: core logic is centralized; subsystem-specific implementations (e.g., Vehicle health sensors) remain in their respective modules.

Vehicle could own health monitoring for its own subsystems (engine, drivetrain, hydraulics), but cannot own the cross-system redundancy logic or maintenance workflow orchestration. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would fragment the availability architecture and create diffusion across Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation is responsible for remote operation and control, not system-wide availability. Assigning DP-D to Teleoperation would create inappropriate coupling between operational control and health management, and would leave Vehicle and Safety Services without clear ownership of their availability contributions.

Safety Services owns fault alerting and safety-critical responses, which are components of the availability architecture. However, Safety Services should not own the full DP-D (health monitoring, redundancy logic, maintenance scheduling) as this would conflate safety management with availability management. Safety Services is a consumer of availability data, not the owner.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner of DP-D because it sits at the system integration level and has visibility and authority over all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It can enforce consistent health monitoring standards, coordinate redundancy activation, and orchestrate maintenance workflows without creating diffusion or inappropriate coupling.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic integration layer, it may become a bottleneck for changes to health monitoring or maintenance logic. Mitigation: define clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Health Monitor, Redundancy Manager, Maintenance Scheduler) with well-defined interfaces.

RISK: Network Link failures could isolate NGHE Solution from telemetry sources, rendering the availability architecture ineffective. Mitigation: implement edge analytics and local caching on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; NGHE Solution must gracefully degrade to local-only diagnostics.

RISK: Maintenance scheduling conflicts with operational tasking could force trade-offs between availability and productivity. Mitigation: establish clear policies and escalation procedures; involve Site Manager and Safety Manager in conflict resolution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution's internal architecture to separate concerns: (1) Health Monitor sub-module (collects and aggregates telemetry), (2) Redundancy Manager sub-module (coordinates failover logic), (3) Maintenance Scheduler sub-module (plans and executes maintenance). Define clear interfaces between these sub-modules and with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Implement Network Link resilience through edge caching and local diagnostics on Vehicle and Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (holistic availability architecture) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The combination of continuous health monitoring, redundancy, and predictive maintenance targets the root causes of unavailability. No fundamental mismatch between problem (FR) and solution (DP).

diffusion_risks: Health monitoring logic could diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if sub-module boundaries are not clearly defined within NGHE Solution. Mitigation: establish Health Monitor as a dedicated sub-module with standardized telemetry interfaces.

Redundancy coordination logic could diffuse if Vehicle and Teleoperation implement their own failover strategies independently. Mitigation: enforce centralized redundancy policy in NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation must delegate failover decisions to NGHE Solution.

Maintenance scheduling could diffuse if Vehicle maintenance is managed separately from Teleoperation maintenance. Mitigation: centralize Maintenance Scheduler in NGHE Solution; all maintenance requests flow through this module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution provides strong containment for DP-D. The module boundary aligns with the system integration level, where cross-subsystem coordination naturally resides. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and manageable. Diffusion risks are primarily internal to NGHE Solution and can be mitigated through sub-module design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR (≥0.97 availability) is feasible through continuous telemetry collection and post-evaluation analysis. NGHE Solution's Health Monitor sub-module will track operational state (active, idle, maintenance, fault) and calculate availability ratio. Verification requires: (1) reliable telemetry transmission (Network Link), (2) accurate time-stamping and state classification, (3) clear definition of 'scheduled operating time' and 'unplanned downtime' with stakeholders.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of availability responsibility is clear: NGHE Solution owns the holistic architecture; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own subsystem-specific implementations (health sensors, redundant controllers, fault alerting). The FR is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner; sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-004) are allocated to appropriate subsystems in the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution: Health Monitor, Redundancy Manager, Maintenance Scheduler. Define interfaces between these sub-modules and with Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services.
Define Network Link resilience strategy: edge caching on Vehicle and Teleoperation, local diagnostics, graceful degradation when Network Link is unavailable.
Establish maintenance scheduling policy: how to resolve conflicts between maintenance and operational tasking; escalation procedures; involvement of Site Manager and Safety Manager.
Define telemetry data standards: what health metrics are collected, at what frequency, with what accuracy and latency requirements.
Plan operator and tele-operator training on new diagnostics and maintenance workflows; include transition period with close monitoring.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and measurable; DP-D is a sound, multi-faceted solution; NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner with manageable containment risks. However, DP-D is composite and requires further decomposition to clarify sub-module boundaries, interface contracts, and implementation details. The sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-004) must be decomposed to assign ownership and design parameters for health monitoring, redundancy, predictive maintenance, and preventive maintenance. Proceeding to the next level will refine the architecture and reduce implementation risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The NGHE solution shall detect and report equipment faults and degradation in real-time via SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces.
FR-AVAIL-002: The NGHE solution shall minimize mean time to repair (MTTR) through predictive maintenance and rapid fault diagnostics.
FR-AVAIL-003: The NGHE solution shall provide redundancy and graceful degradation for critical subsystems to sustain operations during partial failures.
FR-AVAIL-004: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational readiness through scheduled preventive maintenance without exceeding planned downtime budgets.

assumptions: Predictive analytics models can be trained on historical failure data; data quality and representativeness are sufficient.
Redundant subsystems can be implemented within cost, weight, and complexity constraints of heavy equipment.
Network Link is reliable enough to support continuous telemetry; local resilience strategies (edge caching, offline diagnostics) are feasible.
Maintenance scheduling can be coordinated with operational tasking through clear policies and stakeholder involvement.
Operators and tele-operators can be trained on new diagnostics and maintenance workflows within acceptable transition period.
Customer-defined evaluation period is long enough (e.g., ≥3 months) to capture representative availability data and account for seasonal variations.
open_risks: Network Link resilience: if telemetry transmission fails, health monitoring is blind and availability target is at risk. Mitigation: implement edge caching and local diagnostics on Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Maintenance scheduling conflicts: if maintenance is deferred to meet production targets, availability will degrade. Mitigation: establish clear policies and escalation procedures; involve Site Manager and Safety Manager.

Operator training: if operators are not adequately trained on new diagnostics and maintenance workflows, human error could increase downtime. Mitigation: plan comprehensive training and transition period with close monitoring.

Predictive analytics accuracy: if historical failure data is sparse or unrepresentative, prediction accuracy will suffer and MTTR improvements will be limited. Mitigation: start with conservative predictions; refine models over time as data accumulates.

Redundancy cost/complexity: if redundant subsystems are too expensive or complex, they may not be implemented, leaving single points of failure. Mitigation: conduct cost-benefit analysis; prioritize redundancy for highest-impact failure modes.
evidence_needed: Historical failure data for Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems: failure modes, frequencies, durations, root causes.
Network Link reliability metrics: uptime, latency, bandwidth, failure modes.
Maintenance scheduling data: current preventive maintenance intervals, durations, resource requirements.
Operator feedback: current pain points, training needs, acceptance of new diagnostics and maintenance workflows.
Cost and weight constraints for redundant subsystems: what is the acceptable cost/weight premium for availability improvements?
Customer-defined evaluation period and availability target: confirm ≥0.97 is the correct target; clarify measurement basis (scheduled vs. unplanned downtime).
